data.title: Telekom Srbija becomes Platinum Sponsor for EXPO 2027 Belgrade, enhancing digital innovation; plays key role in event's tech.
data.description: Telekom Srbija Group has announced its Platinum Sponsorship of EXPO 2027 Belgrade, showcasing its ongoing commitment to digital innovation. This sponsorship emphasizes the company's role in providing technological infrastructure for the event, ensuring connectivity and support for one of the region's biggest international gatherings. As a central player in EXPO 2027, Telekom Srbija will be instrumental in delivering cutting-edge digital services and reinforcing its leadership in the industry.

data.title: Costamare Bulkers Holdings reports a net loss of $1.7 million in Q4-2025, liquidity of $311 million, $155.6 million debt, and $226.3 million cash. They own 31 vessels, with an average age of 13 years. They sold one vessel for an estimated capital gain of $7 million and agreed to acquire another vessel. They provide further information on financial results, vessel disposals and acquisitions, and fleet details. Call details are scheduled for further discussion.
data.description: Costamare Bulkers Holdings Limited reported unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and year ended December 31, 2025. The company had an adjusted net loss of $1.7 million in Q4 2025 with liquidity of $311.0 million, debt of $155.6 million, and cash of $226.3 million, resulting in a negative net debt position of $70.7 million. The company owns a fleet of 31 dry bulk vessels with a total capacity of approximately 2.8 million DWT. They have entered into agreements for the sale and purchase of vessels, with estimated capital gains. The company's financial summary for 2025 and Q4 2025 includes voyage revenue and adjusted net loss figures. The company also provided operational updates regarding its trading book transfer with Cargill, fleet renewal activities, and owned fleet details. Management discussed the company's performance, market fundamentals, and fleet composition. The article also includes details on non-GAAP financial measures, utilization of owned dry bulk fleet, consolidated financial results, cash flows, and liquidity of the company. Lastly, the article provides information on upcoming conference calls for the company.

data.title: AMD supports $300M loan for Crusoe's AI deployment, follows Nvidia's model. Loan collateralized by AMD chips. Loan interest rate: 6%. Crusoe pivots to AI from crypto.
data.description: The tech company AMD (NASDAQ: AMD) has agreed to secure a $300 million loan for the cloud computing startup Crusoe. In the deal, if Crusoe fails to attract customers like AI developers, AMD will repurchase its chips. The loan, facilitated by Goldman Sachs (NYSE: GS), will be backed by the chips and related equipment, ensuring a low interest rate of approximately 6% due to AMD's involvement. Crusoe, formerly focused on cryptocurrency, has shifted to AI infrastructure and is included in the developing "neoclouds" sector. Currently, AMD chips are being integrated into a data center in Ohio controlled by 5C, a Canadian company supported by Brookfield. Check the complete article on Benzinga.com for more details.

data.title: Bluejay Diagnostics confirms exercising all prefunded warrants from Oct 2025 private placement, now has 972k shares outstanding and 1.5M cash exercisable warrants with $7.00+ price per share.
data.description: In Acton, Massachusetts, on February 20, 2026, Bluejay Diagnostics (NASDAQ: BJDX) made an announcement regarding their prefunded warrants. The company revealed that as of February 19, 2026, all prefunded warrants from the October 2025 private placement have been fully exercised, with no remaining prefunded warrants in any category. Currently, there are approximately 972,000 shares of common stock outstanding. Furthermore, the company also has cash exercisable warrants totaling around 1.5 million shares, each with a cash exercise price of $7.00 or more per share.

data.title: LyondellBasell lowers quarterly dividend to $0.69 per share from $1.37, to be paid on March 9, 2026.
data.description: LyondellBasell declared a $0.69 per share quarterly dividend, a decrease of $0.68 from the previous quarter's dividend. Shareholders as of March 2, 2026, will receive the payment on March 9, 2026. This announcement was made by the board of directors in Houston and London on Feb. 20, 2026.

data.title: Bitcoin ETFs see $166M in outflows, totaling near $4B in 5 weeks. Experts discuss if trend shows reset or weakness.
data.description: In the last five weeks, outflows from Bitcoin ETFs have reached nearly $4 billion. There is currently a debate among experts on whether this streak of outflows indicates a reset in the market or highlights a potential structural weakness in the ETFs.

data.title: Bimergen Energy priced public offering at $4 per share, raising $13.6 million with warrants, each exercisable at $5. Underwriters have option to purchase more shares and warrants.
data.description: On February 20, 2026, Bimergen Energy Corporation (“Bimergen” or the “Company”) (NYSE American: BESS, BESSWS) disclosed the details of its underwritten public offering. The offering consisted of common stock (or pre-funded warrants) along with one warrant per common stock or pre-funded warrant, priced at $4.00 per share. This resulted in gross proceeds amounting to $13,600,000, before accounting for underwriting discounts and offering expenses. Each warrant holds the ability to be exchanged for one share of common stock at an exercise price of $5.00, and can be exercised immediately for five years post-issuance. Moreover, Bimergen has provided the underwriters with a 45-day option to procure up to an additional 200,000 shares of common stock (or pre-funded warrants) and/or 200,000 warrants to cover any over-allotments.

data.title: AngloGold Ashanti reported record $2.9bn free cash flow in 2025 with $875m interim dividend for Q4, totaling $1.8bn for the year.
data.description: In 2025, AngloGold Ashanti plc reported a record free cash flow of $2.9 billion due to robust production growth, cost control, and an increased average gold price per ounce. The company declared an interim dividend of $875 million for Q4 2025, bringing the total payout for the year to $1.8 billion, the largest ever. AngloGold Ashanti met its targets for gold production and sustaining capital expenditure for the year, showcasing its strong performance.
